name: Pan Bagna
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely chop the tomatoes, scallions, red onions, peppers, olives, capers, pickles, garlic, and anchovies.
2. Season the mixture with paprika, salt, and pepper to taste.
3. Add red wine vinegar and olive oil, blending until combined.
4. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
5. Split a long, narrow loaf of French bread in half lengthwise as evenly as possible.
6. Carefully remove the soft bread from the center, leaving a crust wall at least 1/2 inch thick.
7. Process the soft bread until finely chopped and add to the vegetable mixture with an additional 2 tablespoons olive oil.
8. Let rest for 20 minutes, then taste and adjust seasoning by adding more oil if needed.
9. Stuff the loaf tightly with the vegetable/bread mixture, ensuring all interior spaces are filled.
10. Tie the loaf closed with string, wrap in foil, and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.
11. Slice the loaf into 1-inch pieces and serve very cold, offering 2 or 3 slices per person. This is best eaten with a knife and fork. It can also be made in small, individual loaves.
